rule_applications.summary: The endpoint uses POST for a profile update operation, which is less appropriate than PATCH or PUT for updating an existing resource.
The API requires login but does not describe authorization checks to ensure the authenticated user can update the specified user_id.
The response includes sensitive data, including an access_token and phone number.
The error model only defines success and server error, omitting expected client-side errors such as authentication failure, authorization failure, validation failure, and not found.
The request schema does not specify validation constraints for display_name, phone, or avatar_url.
The API returns a custom code/message envelope but does not specify corresponding HTTP status codes.

findings.title: Use PATCH or PUT for profile updates
findings.description: The endpoint updates an existing user profile but uses POST. PATCH is typically preferred for partial updates, while PUT is appropriate for full replacement.
findings.recommendation: Change the method to PATCH /api/v1/users/{user_id}/profile for partial updates, or PUT if the full profile resource is replaced.

findings.title: Potential missing authorization check
findings.description: The endpoint requires login, but the API does not state whether the authenticated user is allowed to update the user_id in the path. This may allow an authenticated user to update another user's profile.
findings.recommendation: Require that user_id matches the authenticated principal, or enforce explicit administrative privileges. Return 403 when the caller is not authorized.

findings.title: Access token exposed in response
findings.description: The response includes access_token: debug-token. Profile update responses should not return authentication tokens unless token issuance is the purpose of the endpoint.
findings.recommendation: Remove access_token from the response. Never return debug tokens or credentials from profile update APIs.

findings.title: Phone number returned in response
findings.description: The response returns the user's phone number, which is personally identifiable information. Returning it may be unnecessary and can increase exposure risk.
findings.recommendation: Return only fields needed by the client. Consider masking the phone number or omitting it unless explicitly required.

findings.title: Incomplete error code definitions
findings.description: Only code 0 and 500 are documented. The API does not define common error cases such as invalid request body, invalid phone format, invalid avatar URL, unauthenticated request, unauthorized access, or missing user.
findings.recommendation: Define and document appropriate HTTP status codes and application error codes, such as 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, and 422.

findings.title: Missing input validation constraints
findings.description: The request fields display_name, phone, and avatar_url do not define required/optional status, length limits, allowed formats, or normalization rules.
findings.recommendation: Specify validation rules, for example display_name length and allowed characters, phone format and region handling, and avatar_url requiring HTTPS with a maximum length.

findings.title: HTTP status code behavior is unspecified
findings.description: The response uses a code/message envelope but does not state whether HTTP status codes are also used consistently.
findings.recommendation: Document HTTP status codes for success and failure. Use HTTP 200 or 204 for successful updates and appropriate 4xx/5xx status codes for errors.
